author: Allah Bakhsh, Maulvi
title: Farhang
gloss: A glossary of difficult words occurring in the First Middle Course, with their meanings in simple Urdu.
publisher: The author, who is Second Master, Middle School, Gulistan, in Balúchistán
notes: Section carries '(Designed for Educational purposes.)'. Author registered from Baluchistan — Punjab registry's catchment beyond the province.

author: Asa Ram, Lala
title: Urdu ka Pahla wa Dusra Qaida
copyright: The Manager, Newal Kishore Press, Lahore; No. 158, dated the 25th October, 1910.
notes: Two primers registered as one entry (Pp. 16 and 24, '6 pies each'), 7,000 copies — primer economics: high volume, low price, corporate copyright by the press manager.

author: Kanshi Ram, Pandit
title: Majma-ul-Asma
gloss: A vocabulary of nouns for the use of rural and zamindari schools.
publisher: The author, who is first teacher in the Primary School, Jassowál
notes: 6th edition village-school vocabulary by a serving primary teacher.

title: Urdu ki Tisri kitab jadid ki Farhang
gloss: A glossary of the difficult words of the new Third Urdu Reader with their meanings in simple Urdu.
publisher: Lala Devi Das and Sons, Book-sellers
notes: Parasite-market glossary keyed to the official 'new Third Urdu Reader' — Text-Book Committee curriculum spawning private study aids (pair with s19).

title: Urdu ki Chhati kitab jadid ki Farhang
gloss: A glossary of the difficult words of the new Sixth Urdu Reader.
publisher: Lala Devi Das and Sons, Book-sellers
notes: Companion to s18; same publisher, same day, consecutive regs 1339–1340.

author: Jawala Singh, B.
title: Urdu Zaban ki Pahli kitab Gurmukhi harfon men
gloss: An Urdu Primer in the Gurmukhi character.
notes: Section carries '(Designed for educational purposes).'. Urdu taught through Gurmukhi script by a serving soldier of the 34th Sikhs — army literacy pipeline crossing the script divide; serial 20 continues the Urdu—Language sequence across the script subsection.

author: Mahbub Beg, Mirza, B.A., LL.B.
title: Urdu Sharh-i-Majmua-i-Qwanin-i-Tazirat-i-Hind
gloss: An Urdu commentary of the Indian Penal Code or Act 45 of 1860.
copyright: M. Mahbub Beg, B.A., LL.B., Pleader, Lahore; No. 182, dated the 21st November, 1910.
notes: IPC commentary in Urdu at 3,000 copies by a Lahore pleader — colonial law vernacularized and copyrighted within weeks of publication.
